name: Tibetan Go (Mig-mang)
author: Abstract Games factory
description: The traditional Tibetan form of Go (mig-mang), per John Fairbairn's 2005 GoGoD account: 17x17 board, a fixed 12-stone third-line setup, White moves first, and one special rule — you may never play immediately on a point just vacated by a capture (kos, snapbacks and throw-ins must all wait one move). Chinese area scoring, komi 0, with a +20 zi bonus for controlling all four corner 1-1 points and +5 zi more for the centre.
